title: Video-to-World Multiview Generation
init_flow: START: __init__(inference_type, checkpoint_dir, checkpoint_name, n_views=6, num_input_frames=1, offload_network, offload_tokenizer, ...)
Set model_name = MODEL_NAME_DICT[checkpoint_name], guidance, num_steps, height, width, fps, num_video_frames, seed
if not disable_guardrail and not offload_guardrail_models: _load_text_guardrail(), _load_video_guardrail()
Back in T2W: if enable_prompt_upsampler and not offload: _load_prompt_upsampler_model()
generate_activity_flow: → text_encoder.encode_prompts([p]) for each prompt → embeddings list, masks list
num_video_frames = self.num_video_frames * len(prompt_embedding),  // total frames = frames * n_views
STEP 3c — Tokenizer encoding (condition latent from input video):
→ model.decode(sample) → raw video [B, 3, T, H, W]
→ rearrange: 'b c (v t) h w -> b c v t h w' with v = n_views
→ reorder views: [1, 0, 2, 4, 3, 5] (front, left, right, back, back_left, back_right → grid order)
→ rearrange grid: 'b c (h w) t h1 w1 -> b c t (h h1) (w w1)' with h=2, w=3 (2x3 grid)
→ convert to uint8 numpy: shape [T, H*2, W*3, C]

offload_branching.offload_tokenizer: Load tokenizer before encoding/decoding, delete + gc after
offload_branching.offload_network: Load diffusion network just before _run_model, delete + gc after
offload_branching.offload_text_encoder_model: Load T5 encoder before embedding, delete + gc after
offload_branching.offload_guardrail_models: Load guardrail models on demand, delete + gc after each check
offload_branching.note: Multiview generate() does NOT run guardrail checks (unlike parent DiffusionVideo2WorldGenerationPipeline)

key_differences_from_parent_v2w: generate() accepts prompt as dict with 6 keys (front, left, right, back, back_left, back_right)
_run_tokenizer_decoding() produces [grid_video, video] list — 2x3 view grid + individual views
No guardrail checks on prompt or output video in generate()

method_signatures.generate: (prompt: dict, image_or_video_path: str) → tuple[list[np.ndarray], dict] | None

important_notes: n_views=6 (front, front_left, front_right, back, back_left, back_right)
Output is 2x3 grid video + per-view video tensor
num_video_frames is multiplied by n_views for total frame count: num_video_frames * 6
condition_latent from tokenizer encoding conditions the diffusion model on input video frames
T5 encoder processes each of the 6 view prompts independently
Video decoding: normalizes (1+x)/2, rearranges spatiotemporal dims for grid layout
